# Rename Chroma store to unified; migrate + drop stale memory-core

## Context

Sub of #12153 — the core change: make the persist dir a single flat `unified` store, identical local + cloud; migrate existing data; delete the federated-leftover `memory-core` folder. Builds on the `#12152` SSOT.

## The Architectural Reality

- **SSOT** `ai/config.template.mjs` `engines.chroma.dataDir` (`#12152`) — flip value `…/chroma/knowledge-base` → `…/chroma/unified`.
- **Local daemon launch** — orchestrator `ai/daemons/orchestrator/TaskDefinitions.mjs` chroma `--path` → `.neo-ai-data/chroma/unified` (SSOT-sourced, or kept-in-sync literal per `#12152`'s resilience note).
- **Cloud** — `ai/deploy/docker-compose.yml` (+ `.dev.yml`/`.test.yml`) `chroma-data:/chroma/chroma` → `/chroma/unified` + the image persist-dir env.
- **One-time migration** — relocate the live served store's contents into `unified`; delete the stale `chroma/memory-core/` folder.

## The Fix

- Config SSOT value → `unified`.
- Orchestrator `--path` → `unified`.
- Compose volume mounts → `/chroma/unified` (all three compose files) + persist-dir env.
- One-shot idempotent migration under `ai/scripts/migrations/` (snapshot-protected) to move live data and remove the stale `memory-core` folder. *(structural-pre-flight applies to the new `.mjs`.)*

## Contract Ledger

| Surface | Authority | Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `engines.chroma.dataDir` | `config.template.mjs` | `…/chroma/unified` | none — SSOT | ADR + Configuration.md | `#12152` |
| cloud chroma volume mount | `docker-compose*.yml` | `/chroma/unified` | n/a | ADR | `docker-compose.yml:22` |
| orchestrator chroma `--path` | `TaskDefinitions.mjs` | `unified` | n/a | ADR | `TaskDefinitions.mjs` |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] SSOT + orchestrator `--path` + all compose files resolve to `unified`; no `knowledge-base`/`memory-core` persist paths remain.
- [ ] Migration relocates live data into `unified` and deletes the stale `memory-core` (snapshot first).
- [ ] Post-migration: HealthService `topology: 'unified'`; KB + MC connect and search green.
- [ ] Depends-on `#12152` (SSOT merged first).

## Out of Scope

defrag (parent sub 3), release scripts (parent sub 4), docs (parent sub 5).

## Related

#12153 (parent), #12152 (depends-on).

Origin Session ID: efd8dc2e-2052-4089-814a-ab22cd8c6a62

- 2026-05-28T23:44:28Z @neo-opus-ada marked this issue as blocking #12156
- 2026-05-28T23:52:38Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `d1b5107` - "fix(ai): drop half-honored NEO_CHROMA_DATA_DIR override (#12142)

@neo-gpt's PR #12152 review caught a real contract split: the NEO_CHROMA_DATA_DIR
env override was honored by the KB/MC server configs + defragChromaDB, but the
orchestrator's chroma daemon --path is a fixed literal that ignores it. Under
override the maintenance scripts would target a dir the live daemon never persists
to — re-introducing the exact wrong-dir bug this PR set out to fix.

Remove the override surface: engines.chroma.dataDir is now a fixed canonical persist
dir (the default value is unchanged). All consumers — both server configs,
defragChromaDB, and the local orchestrator daemon-launch literal — resolve to the
same fixed path, so the split is structurally impossible. Tighten the docstring to
stop overclaiming daemon-aligned SSOT-ness.

Wiring the orchestrator --path through the resolved value (so a future override is
honored end-to-end across the daemon too) lands with the unified-store rename epic
#12153 (sub #12155).

Tests: the engines.chroma assertion drops the env-override branch; the TIER1_DEFAULTS
fixture pins the fixed path. config.template + KB/MC config.template specs green
(13 passed).

Co-Authored-By: neo-opus-ada <neo-opus-ada@neomjs.com>"
- 2026-05-28T23:54:04Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #12152
- 2026-05-29T00:09:36Z @tobiu referenced in commit `e1b3d7a` - "fix(ai): defrag-memory uses the unified Chroma persist-dir SSOT (#12142) (#12152)

* fix(ai): defrag-memory uses the unified Chroma persist-dir SSOT (#12142)

ai:defrag-memory targeted a stale, server-local '.neo-ai-data/chroma/memory-core'
dir (the MC config dataDir), but the unified store (ADR 0003) is one daemon + one
persist dir — the KB's '.neo-ai-data/chroma/knowledge-base'. So defrag-memory
operated on the wrong directory (and hard-exited when it was absent).

Add AiConfig.engines.chroma.dataDir as the single source of truth for the unified
persist dir (= the current live value; env NEO_CHROMA_DATA_DIR), and have the KB
config path + MC config dataDir + (transitively) the defrag adapters read it. MC's
dataDir now resolves to the unified store, so ai:defrag-memory targets the live
store. No consumer's resolved dir changes — the SSOT equals the prior KB value.

The orchestrator chroma --path is intentionally kept a relative literal (with a
cross-reference comment), NOT reading the SSOT: a deployment whose config.mjs is
stale (lacking the new key) would otherwise launch the daemon with
`--path undefined`. The server/defrag reads only affect the physical/maintenance
path, never the live daemon launch — so this scope is the safe one.

Deployment note: existing config.mjs copies must be re-hydrated from the template
to pick up engines.chroma.dataDir (the established template-addition migration).
Shadow-swap robustness for the (manual, snapshot-protected, #12140-safe) MC defrag
remains a tracked follow-up.

Tests: engines.chroma SSOT assertion (config.template.spec + TIER1_DEFAULTS
fixture); MC config.template.spec now asserts dataDir == the unified SSOT (was a
stale-path regression anchor). All affected config specs green (template-based,
harness-independent).

Co-Authored-By: neo-opus-ada <neo-opus-ada@neomjs.com>

* docs(ai): drop decay-prone ticket-id refs from SSOT comments (#12142)

JS comments must not carry ticket IDs — they are tracking anchors that decay
into noise once merged (load-bearing refs belong in PR bodies, commit subjects,
and test.describe labels). Removes the five `#12142` refs that 591746914
introduced into the unified Chroma persist-dir SSOT comments (config.template,
KB/MC server configs, orchestrator TaskDefinitions) + the MC config.template
spec, preserving the evergreen WHY in each.

Comment-only; no behavior change. config.template + MC config specs green
(11 passed).

Co-Authored-By: neo-opus-ada <neo-opus-ada@neomjs.com>

* fix(ai): drop half-honored NEO_CHROMA_DATA_DIR override (#12142)

@neo-gpt's PR #12152 review caught a real contract split: the NEO_CHROMA_DATA_DIR
env override was honored by the KB/MC server configs + defragChromaDB, but the
orchestrator's chroma daemon --path is a fixed literal that ignores it. Under
override the maintenance scripts would target a dir the live daemon never persists
to — re-introducing the exact wrong-dir bug this PR set out to fix.

Remove the override surface: engines.chroma.dataDir is now a fixed canonical persist
dir (the default value is unchanged). All consumers — both server configs,
defragChromaDB, and the local orchestrator daemon-launch literal — resolve to the
same fixed path, so the split is structurally impossible. Tighten the docstring to
stop overclaiming daemon-aligned SSOT-ness.

Wiring the orchestrator --path through the resolved value (so a future override is
honored end-to-end across the daemon too) lands with the unified-store rename epic
#12153 (sub #12155).

Tests: the engines.chroma assertion drops the env-override branch; the TIER1_DEFAULTS
fixture pins the fixed path. config.template + KB/MC config.template specs green
(13 passed).

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"
- 2026-05-29T00:28:58Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by #12153
- 2026-05-29T00:45:49Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #12159
- 2026-05-29T01:18:35Z @tobiu referenced in commit `7cedc3b` - "docs(ai): ADR 0017 — single flat unified Chroma store + dev/prod parity (#12154) (#12159)

Adds ADR 0017 amending ADR 0003 (Chroma Topology — Unified Only): preserves the
one-daemon core decision and records the store's concrete shape — a single flat
persist store named `unified`, identical local and cloud, with KB/MC/tenant
separation enforced by collection + metadata (isolation), collection-scoped
export (shipping), and the KB-as-cache/MC-as-store backup model (recovery) —
never by directory or daemon split. Retires the federated-era two-folder local
layout (`knowledge-base/` + stale `memory-core/`).

Grounded in a 6-agent plan stress-test of Epic #12153 + a live store/daemon/
cloud-compose audit. Encodes the verified cloud fact (the chromadb image
persists to WORKDIR-relative /chroma/chroma; PERSIST_DIRECTORY is mandatory to
redirect it) and the per-collection-HNSW rationale (one store does not degrade
per-collection search).

Forward-links: ADR 0003 gains an "Amended by ADR 0017" row; ADR 0014 §2.5's
"ADR 0003 needs no amendment" sweep gets a dated forward note (its shared-
primitive / one-container classification is unaffected).

The code subs implement against this record: #12155 (rename+migrate), #12156
(defrag), #12157 (release-privacy), #12158 (guides).

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"
- 2026-05-29T01:29:31Z @neo-opus-ada assigned to @neo-opus-ada
- 2026-05-29T01:55:10Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #12162
- 2026-05-29T02:08:33Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `d3624c7` - "refactor(ai): trim #12155 to rename-only — drop the one-time migration framework (#12155)

Per operator review: the migration script + spec + orchestrator strand-guard
(~1000 lines) were over-engineered for what is a ONE-TIME `mv` on the single
machine that exists (no cloud deployment yet; the swarm + operator are the only
Neo users). After the single run, all of it would be permanent dead code.

Removes:
- ai/scripts/migrations/migrateChromaToUnified.mjs + its spec
- the `ai:migrate-chroma-unified` package.json script
- the orchestrator strand-guard (detectChromaStrandRisk + the Orchestrator.start
  guard + its tests) — Orchestrator.mjs + its spec reverted to dev

Keeps the durable rename only: the `engines.chroma.dataDir` SSOT value, the
orchestrator chroma `--path`, the `ai:server` script, the three compose files
(`/chroma/unified` + `PERSIST_DIRECTORY`), and the config test specs + fixtures.
The one-time local data move is a documented manual operator step (see the PR
body), not shipped code.
